title: n-PCR for a single sample
author: Matias Ruggieri
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zjyf4pw

steps.title: Equipment and Materials Pipettes 10µl presterilized...
steps.instruction: Equipment and Materials Pipettes 10µl presterilized filter tips 20µl presterilized filter tips 200µl presterilized filter tips 1000µl presterilized filter tips Thermal Cycle (we used TProfessional TRIO) Electrophoresis equipment

steps.title: Reagents and Chemicals Taq DNA Polymerase...
steps.instruction: Reagents and Chemicals Taq DNA Polymerase kit (1000 U), by Qiagen , Catalog #: Cat No./ID: 201205 Buffers (PCR Buffer and CoralLoad PCR Buffer) MgCl2 Q solution Taq polymerase dNTPs Nuclease free H2O Forward (F) and reverse (R) primers Agarose Markers: 50pb and/or 100pb (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Germany)

steps.title: PCR summary protocol Primary PCR/ Nest-1...
steps.instruction: PCR summary protocol Primary PCR/ Nest-1 PCR should be done using PolEF and PolER primers. For each group of samples include positive control (DNA from MT2 cell line) and a negative control of PCR mix without a DNA template. Nest-2 PCR should be done using the primers PolIF and PolIR. Also the primers Actin F and Actin R are necessary to amplify human Actin gene. Once the PCR run is complete, remove tubes and briefly centrifuge to spin down products. Store PCR tubes in the fridge or freezer Run the samples on 2% Agarose gel electrophoresis using gel red nucleic acid stain. Score results as presence or absence of bands comparing with control bands.

steps.title: PCR Protocol For each run, at...
steps.instruction: PCR Protocol For each run, at least two controls (1 positive and 1 negative) should be included in addition to the samples. Prepare the PCR master mix calculating enough volume for number of the samples +8%, to account for pipetting loss and dead volume. E.g. for 22 samples, one positive control and one negative control (total of 24 tubes), make a master mix for 26 reactions. Master Mix for Nest-1 PCR ( 10X PCR buffer , dNTPs, MgCl2, primers, Taq polymerase and water) Master Mix for Nest-2 PCR ( 10X CoralLoad PCR Buffer , dNTPs, MgCl2, primers, Taq polymerase and water)

steps.title: eparation of PCR (Nest-1 PCR) %s...
steps.instruction: eparation of PCR (Nest-1 PCR) %s %s Preparation of PCR (Nest-2 PCR) %s %s

steps.title: PCR Cycling conditions Nest-1
steps.instruction: PCR Cycling conditions Nest-1 : Pol EF + Pol ER %s %s Nest-2 : Pol IF + Pol IR + Act F + Acr R %s %s

steps.title: Preparation and storage of reagents Stock...
steps.instruction: Preparation and storage of reagents Stock reagents for PCR (10X buffer, dNTPs, MgCl2, Q solution and primers) and enzymes should be stored at -20°C. Stock reagents should be diluted into working concentrations and aliquoted at -20°C into separate tubes. Working aliquots of sample and control DNA as well as primary PCR (Nest-1) products must be kept at -20°C for long-term storage but can be kept in the fridge for daily use.
